title: A generation of men raised by women. Fight Club

sentences.en: If you could fight anyone, who would you fight?
sentences.ko: 아무나 싸울 수 있다면, 누구랑 싸울 거야?

sentences.en: Yeah, why? Who would you fight? Fight my dad.
sentences.ko: 응, 왜? 너는 누구랑 싸울 건데? 우리 아버지.

sentences.en: I mean, I know him, but he left when I was, like, six years old.
sentences.ko: 아, 알긴 아는데, 내가 여섯 살쯤에 떠났어.

sentences.en: Married this other woman and had some other kids.
sentences.ko: 다른 여자랑 결혼하고 다른 애들도 낳았지.

sentences.en: And he, like, did this every six years. He goes to a new city and starts a new family.
sentences.ko: 6년마다 이걸 반복해. 새로운 도시에 가서 새 가족을 만들어.

sentences.en: My dad never went to college, so it was real important that I go.
sentences.ko: 우리 아버지는 대학을 안 가서, 내가 가는 게 진짜 중요했어.

sentences.en: That sounds familiar. So I graduate. I call him up long distance.
sentences.ko: 나도 비슷해. 그래서 졸업하고 장거리 전화를 걸었어.

sentences.en: I say, Dad, now what? He says, get a job.
sentences.ko: 아빠, 이제 뭐 해? 하니까, 취직해, 라고 하더라.

sentences.en: Same here. Now I'm 25. I make my yearly call again.
sentences.ko: 나도 그래. 이제 25살이 됐어. 또 일 년에 한 번 전화를 걸지.

sentences.en: I say, Dad, now what? He says, I don't know. Get married.
sentences.ko: 아빠, 이제 뭐 해? 하니까, 모르겠다, 결혼해, 라고 하더라.

sentences.en: Dad, I mean, you can't get married.
We're a generation of men raised by women.
I'm wondering if another woman is really the answer we need.
sentences.ko: 또 다른 여자가 정말 우리에게 필요한 답인지 모르겠어.
